name: Maine School of Science and Mathematics
about: The Maine School of Science and Mathematics (MSSM) is a public residential magnet high school in Limestone, Maine, established in 1995. It is a 100% boarding, STEM-focused school serving grades 9-12 with an intensive curriculum in mathematics, science, technology, and the humanities. Maine residents pay no tuition (only room and board); 100% of graduates matriculate to four-year colleges.
college_matriculation: 100% of MSSM graduates matriculate to a four-year college
financial_aid_notes: No tuition for Maine residents (room and board ~$8,300). Non-residents pay tuition (~$34,300) plus room and board (~$9,300) and a non-resident fee. MSSM Foundation Student Aid Program provides need-based assistance.
extracurriculars: Athletics shared with Limestone Community School

name: Garrison Forest School
about: Garrison Forest School is an independent, non-denominational college-preparatory girls' school founded in 1910 on a 110-acre campus in Owings Mills, Maryland. It serves girls from kindergarten through grade 12 (with a coeducational early childhood program) and offers a boarding program for grades 9-12. The school is nationally known for its riding program and is the only U.S. girls' school offering polo.
college_matriculation: 98% college acceptance; top destinations include University of Maryland, University of Virginia, and Syracuse University
accreditation: Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools; Association of Independent Maryland Schools (AIMS)
notable_alumni: Beth Botsford (Olympic gold medalist swimmer), Patricia E. Campbell-Smith (federal judge), Cricket Hooper Jiranek (Tony Award-winning Broadway producer)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial assistance is available; the school participates in standard need-based aid programs.

name: Georgetown Preparatory School
about: Georgetown Preparatory School is America's oldest Catholic boarding and day school for boys, founded in 1789, and the only Jesuit boarding school in the United States. Situated on 93 acres in North Bethesda, Maryland (suburban Washington, D.C.), it serves grades 9-12 with both day and boarding students. Its Jesuit mission emphasizes intellectual excellence, reflection, and forming men of competence, conscience, courage, and compassion.
college_matriculation: 100% college acceptance; top destinations include Georgetown University, University of Notre Dame, and the University of Maryland Honors Program
accreditation: Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools
notable_alumni: Brett Kavanaugh (U.S. Supreme Court Justice), Neil Gorsuch (U.S. Supreme Court Justice), Jerome Powell (Federal Reserve Chair), Ian Harding (actor)
financial_aid_notes: About 29% of families receive financial aid; day student grants range from $5,000 to $45,000.
head_of_school: John Glennon (Headmaster); Rev. James R. Van Dyke, S.J. (President)

name: McDonogh School
about: McDonogh School is an independent, non-denominational, coeducational school founded in 1873 on an 800-acre campus in Owings Mills, Maryland. Serving grades PK-12 with about 1,460 students, it became coeducational in 1975 and offers a five-day boarding program for a small number of Upper School students. The school has moved beyond the AP curriculum, allowing faculty to design advanced courses, with honors or advanced offerings in all academic departments.
accreditation: Association of Independent Maryland Schools (AIMS); National Association of Independent Schools (NAIS)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid is offered to qualifying families.
head_of_school: David J. Farace (13th Head of School, since 2018)
athletic_conference: Maryland Interscholastic Athletic Association (MIAA); Interscholastic Athletic Association of Maryland (IAAM)

name: Chapel Hill - Chauncy Hall School
about: Chapel Hill-Chauncy Hall School (CH-CH) is an independent, non-denominational, college-preparatory day and boarding school on a roughly 40-acre campus in Waltham, Massachusetts, about ten miles from Boston. It serves grades 8-12 plus a postgraduate year, with about 200 students (45% boarding, 25% international). The school was formed in 1971 when Chauncy Hall (founded 1828) merged with Chapel Hill, and it incorporates a Multiple Intelligences approach to teaching.
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC)
notable_alumni: Alice Stone Blackwell (suffragist), Curtis Guild Jr. (43rd Governor of Massachusetts), Lucy Wheelock (founder of Wheelock College)
financial_aid_notes: The school awards over $1 million in need-based financial aid to more than 25% of its students each year. A $5,000 enrollment deposit is required from domestic students and $14,000 from international students.
athletic_conference: Massachusetts Bay Independent League (MBIL); Independent Girls' Conference (IGC); Eastern Independent League (EIL)
ap_courses: AP courses available across major disciplines

name: Concord Academy
about: Concord Academy is an independent, coeducational, college-preparatory day and boarding school in historic Concord, Massachusetts, founded in 1922 (incorporated; classes began 1919) and coeducational since 1971. It serves about 419 students in grades 9-12 on a 39-acre campus, with 40% boarding and 11% international students. The school eliminated AP courses in 2005 in favor of faculty-designed advanced courses and meets 100% of demonstrated financial need.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend selective colleges including Ivy League and other top universities; notable alumni include a Harvard University president
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC)
notable_alumni: Drew Gilpin Faust (28th President of Harvard), Sarah Koenig (host of Serial podcast), Caroline Kennedy (former U.S. Ambassador)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid covers 100% of demonstrated need for admitted students.
head_of_school: Henry Fairfax (Head of School, since July 2022)
